candidates.name: Meeting Recap
candidates.workflow: After every customer call, the CSM writes a recap and next steps into the CRM by hand. Transcripts already exist; nobody reads them.
candidates.inputs: Call transcript (exists today), account ID, attendee list
candidates.outputs: Structured recap — summary, action items, risk flags, next-step date — posted as a CRM activity
guardrails: Output validator: recap must match the CRM activity schema
Escalation: action items mentioning contract, refund, or legal terms route to a human first
candidates.success_metric: % of calls with a CRM recap within 1 hour (target 95%; today ~40% within 3 days)
candidates.value_basis: 320 calls/mo x 12 min x $45/hr loaded CSM cost
candidates.cost_basis: 320 runs x ~12k in / 0.6k out on Sonnet
candidates.build_vs_buy: CRM vendors sell call-summary add-ons (~$30/seat/mo, ~$1,500/mo at this seat count) that summarize but don't follow the team's recap schema or escalate. Build: the prompt is one page and the transcript pipeline already exists.

candidates.name: RFP Response Drafter
candidates.workflow: Each inbound RFP gets a first-draft response assembled by an AE from past responses, the security doc, and product sheets — 90 minutes of copy-paste archaeology per RFP.
candidates.trigger: human request — AE submits the RFP, agent drafts section by section (~8 sections per RFP)
candidates.inputs: RFP document, response library (needs consolidating — see data readiness), product/security fact sheets
candidates.outputs: Per-section first drafts with citations into the response library; AE reviews and finalizes
guardrails: Output validator: every claim must cite a response-library source — uncited claims are blocked
Completion checker: max 2 revision passes per section
Escalation: pricing and legal sections always route to a human
candidates.success_metric: AE time per RFP (target: 90 min -> 25 min review)
candidates.value_basis: 40 RFPs/mo x 90 min x $60/hr loaded AE cost
candidates.cost_basis: ~8 section-runs per RFP x 40 RFPs x ~32k in / 3k out on Sonnet
candidates.build_vs_buy: RFP-automation vendors exist (Loopio-class, ~$15-20k/yr) and are good at the library workflow; weak at company-voice drafting. Buy the library if RFP volume doubles; build the drafter either way — it rides on the same response library.

candidates.name: Churn-Signal Digest
candidates.workflow: Once a week an analyst assembles a churn-risk digest by eyeballing product usage dashboards, support ticket tone, and invoice status across ~600 accounts.
candidates.trigger: schedule — daily account-batch scans, weekly digest assembly
candidates.inputs: Usage metrics export, support ticket summaries, billing status (three systems, no joined view today)
candidates.outputs: Weekly ranked digest: top 15 at-risk accounts with the evidence trail per account
guardrails: Output validator: every flagged account must list its evidence sources
Rate limit: batch scans capped at trigger volume
candidates.success_metric: Analyst assembly time (4 hrs -> 30 min review) and % of churned accounts that were flagged in a prior digest
candidates.value_basis: 4 digests/mo x 4 hrs x $42.50/hr analyst cost (value rises if flag precision proves out — score that later, not now)
candidates.cost_basis: 60 runs/mo x ~20k in / 2k out on Opus (cross-source reasoning)
candidates.build_vs_buy: Customer-success platforms (Gainsight-class) do health scores but need the same data plumbing this needs anyway, at ~$30k/yr. Build the digest on the joined data; revisit buying when CS headcount doubles.

candidates.name: Contract Redline Assistant
candidates.workflow: Inbound vendor/customer contracts get redlined by outside counsel at $90/hr equivalent; turnaround is the real complaint.
candidates.inputs: Contract document, playbook of acceptable positions (does not exist in writing today)
candidates.outputs: Suggested redlines with rationale, for counsel review
guardrails: Human review mandatory on every output — no agent-finalized language
Escalation: anything off-playbook goes straight to counsel
candidates.cost_basis: 45 runs/mo x ~25k in / 3k out on Opus
candidates.build_vs_buy: Deliberately ranked low: error tolerance is low, the playbook the agent needs doesn't exist in writing, and a wrong redline has real blast radius. Write the playbook first; rescore next quarter.
